input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10.

This is a known change in .NET MAUI. In .NET 10 (which targets Android 15+ / API 35), edge-to-edge display is enforced by default, and the old `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` API was deprecated in Android API 30 and removed/ignored in API 35.

The solution in .NET 10 is to use the new `KeyboardInsets` approach or handle `WindowInsets` properly. Let me provide a concise, accurate answer.


In .NET 10, `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` no longer works because Android 15 enforces edge-to-edge layouts and that flag was deprecated years ago. The new approach is to use `WindowInsets` listeners or MAUI's built-in keyboard inset handling to adjust the layout when the IME appears. The manifest approach still works, but on Android 15+ the window doesn't physically resize anymore—instead you get inset callbacks that need handling. So I need to either go with `AdjustPan` for simplicity, or use `AdjustResize` paired with proper inset management to manually adjust the layout when the keyboard appears.
